original text:
The new version includes many new exciting functions (RBL, RWL, Automatic white listing) and gives you very granular control over how the filter works. And it’s still available free version!
and have look!.
Since have had lot problems with false positives with the black lists that I’m using Exchange 2003 server started looking into another way filtering spam.
The obvious choice additional protection fell greylisting you can read more about what here
The problem with this that there doesn’t seem any free products out there for Exchange and don’t want set Linux box yet another box the rack decided write one myself.
Usually receive 3500-4000 spam attempts per day that means that mails day are slipping trough. These get matched blacklist that not that aggressive and the result this that spam level has gone down almost while haven’t had single false positive yet.
About the program. consists two parts.
Greylist installs .dll and connects the SMTP service’s RCPT. reads configuration from Greylist.cfg and uses Greylist.mdb for logging entries. also produces log file the log directory.
Greylist admin creates and configures the above files well controls the settings and the white list.
Continue blocking for minutes.
Block Source IP, Sender email address, Recipient address all together any combination.
White list (always allow) Source IP, Sender email address, Recipient address any combination.
Clean out entries older then days the first session the day.
Stores data Microsoft access database, .mdb MsSQL db.
Configures: Block for minutes, Max age days, White list.
Configures which items use when blocking Source IP, Sender email address, Recipient address all together any combination.
Displays blocked items and passed items totals.
Displays current items database.
Displays block rate according all entries the database.
Registered users can now set how Greylist will behave blocked item. can either send the 451 message and then disconnect (default) keep the session alive and accept more recipients.
Registered users can now change the message Greylist sends out. This especially great for corporate usage where each company can have custom message.
For the rest Greylist stays the same for unregistered users.
Added new tab with controls for Disconnect 451 and 451 message.
Even though Greylist has been succeeded JEP(S), the download links remain here for reference.
For comments like ‘Hey great app!’ use the form the end the page.
Nada. Nothing. It’s for free!
See contribution better world free contribution! I’ve released this under Creative Commonce license, which comes down that you can use and redistribute long you refer and this site while using any part program. The full license available the readme file.
Register it! will cost you euro (about USD) and will support the continued development and you’ll get access the customization options for how Greylist behaves the communication level.
The registration license will mailed you soon I’ve registered the payment.
And your boss wants invoice problem! I’ll mail that you upon request.
The program distributed ‘as-is’ and don’t intend provide any support for it.
But feel free send any suggestions improvements your own modifications.
remote SSE install when the 3rd party vendors couldn’t get their own product work.
they released SSE market their isn’t any documentation!! Lucky us.
are onto terrific little applet here.
have been using Greylist for couple weeks now and have found very useful, has blocked 180,000 messages since we’ve started using and has stopped lot emails that our GFI was failing block.
One thing think could changed the way the database cleanup applied. don’t have the address checking enabled then over time you will get spam from/to the same causing all future spam from such pairs get through.
think it’s save assume that the First Seen and Last Seen timestamps are the same and over hours old then not going resent and probably spam. The clean function could look for this and delete those entries, the database would much smaller then. have added query the Access database this for me.
Following from this, there easy way compact the database? Access especially wasteful with regards deleted records and find the database can grow 100MB couple days. run compact the database having disable Greylist remove the file locks and then enable again afterwards.
came across this tool and looks good tool. There one issue faced. After applying this exchange server, had socket errors when trying the email test dnsreport.com restarted the server and went fine. Maybe mandatory step and has manual for product.
Usually own email account got about 50-100 spam emails each day. used run with the GFI sollution, but kept crashing our server, and let quite few span-emails through. This has let spam-mails through during week period! running with the built-in exchange “intelligent filter” along side with greylisting, and have made short “whitelist” domains, and works perfect!
you are using the SQL DSN and you lose connection your server, what happens? Does the software fail back the Jet locally? Does stop greylisting until the connection restored?
that happens then will fallback not blocking greylisting emails until the database connection restored. will try restore the connection every couple minutes automatically. Then will log these error sessions with code 999 the logfile.
This has been tested quiet extensively make sure that works correctly and does.
What’s changed from 1.3.0 1.3.1?
Gmail accounts seem blocked completly. When resends the first and last timestamps are always the same. I’ll update later this changes for me. Also there way track which email are passed?
Micheal: I’ve posted reply your question the community forum here.
Ahh Nevermind. Gmail uses multiple servers, blocking address kills these for while.
from the /23 part, you generate the subnet mask. This unsigned bit integer with the upmost bits set.
Thanks! I’ve choosen another path though.
I’m not converting the IP’s serials and then more then less then comparison between the source ip.
This will implemented the next major version which ‘might’ come month so.
1,000,000 spams stopped and counting!
Chris, just thought I’d pop you quick note. We’ve got the registered version running dozen servers now just our top servers have prevented over 1,000,000 spam emails reaching the servers.
for the latest (couple days ago) Trojan that trying spread email well, all we’ve noticed the greylist logs are little bigger.
Thank-you very much for your wonderfull tool: really works expecded stopping tons spam mails per day.
Can this configured that only works one domain? host mail, not want annoy customers while test this.
Heyden Kirk: Unfortunately not. The next version (2.0) which still under development will have function for ‘learning’. This means that does all the processing while mails are blocked that the filter gets know you senders email patterns.
had slight issue with the greylist program recently, was running amazing for about week and then all sudden stopped allowing email get through. disabled and then re-enabled see that made difference, disabled, deleted the directory downloaded new copy and started and same thing, full blocking all email the server. there anything can check because while worked, ran like champ.
Problem is, this way not able identify the original user who sending email gmail account. For example Sue sends email gmail account and gets forwarded test domain account will have the same format the database mentioned earlier. Since from the database would never know that email came from Sue, what would the way identify the original user the email?
guess was that since gmail account already been whitelisted, when Sue sends email really dosent matter whitelist her email separately because gmail account already the whitelist.
apologize language here bit confusing. Any help this would appreciated. would love implement this real environment.
have problem get e-mail through while having greylist enabled. running version 1.3.1.
have tried disable and then enable again. don’t know what do. have also tried download new copy.
Greylist have worked great for about months, but now something have happened.
have had some problems with this certain customers servers. Here are some tips help those you trouble.
After creating database and enabling the Greylist sink, sometimes all mail will blocked. Greylist admin will also report nothing.
There another problem where you have run the enable command too many times. This creates more than one sink. have found this huge problem and not even realising it.
Hi. Just added this great tool site. look fine, but realized after 1-2 hours that was not happy talk some mail servers. the log said “SMTP 250”, but the sending server said “host dropped connection”. tried everything the above things. last had drop using it. Any ideas
P.S. Really sorry about that because greylisting smashing good techique.
also noticed that messages with status 250 log file are dropped.
has someone any ideas fix this bug
This mails are “return receipt, delivered read messages” and JEPs are blocking this messages.
think that huge bug.
Lars: Regarding the ‘interesting problem’; it’s not lot message you see, but the autowhitelist action.
When you have active then when outbound mail sent, then the recipient email address gets added the inbound sender whitelist. This gets logged the listener like that.
After hours then the sender email address gets automatically removed.
Thus, not bug it’s feature.
would just like say thanks for taking the time write one the most amazing programs I’ve ever seen.
I’m planning registering our copy soon can get approved.
have just installed this server running 2003 and Exchange 2003. Emails are coming straight from everywhere with delay all. system also running Symantec’s Mail Security for Exchange, need uninstall this first?? Also, when telnet port the server still appears that Exchange service listening, should the SMTP banner look different?
Seems like it’s not activated. Have you enabled it?
Normally Greylist works fine with other spam products, but ofcourse there’s guarantee. know that other people use Symantec’s products together with Greylist.
The banner should not change it’s not replacing the IIS SMTP service.
More questions? Look the Greylist forum.
You rock! Thanks for the great tool. solid Directory Harvesting Attack killer what was looking for.
This looks great. Thanks, and I’m looking forward trying this out.
have been using version 2.0 for almost over weeks and works great. Blocks tons spam and very very easy configure. did not see anything that would say wrong the moment. Cant wait for the final version.
know you said you are 95% done but how much time span are looking at?
Enterprise Sales Partner, Corel and Novell Business Partner.).
directly from you. Please write the terms reselling and prices too.
could pay with credit card. Please write your fax number too.
order, could you make out the invoice us, NOT for the enduser?
Forum not accepting new registrations process fails when tries send you your confirmation email. looks like the author’s greylist package stopping the mail?
the Hungarian direct partner ACDSystems, Ulead, WinZip, StarNet etc.).
This important for us.
used greylist linux, now Exchanger can use it. Thanks for your job.
will there ‘upgrade license’? we’ve paied for 1.3 just some weeks ago and were pretty satisfied with the solution. now we’re wondering everybody has pay these â‚¬ 150
Christian: worries you already have Greylist license then you will recieve cupon code that can used for getting â‚¬50 reduction JEP(S). These codes will sent out the next coming weeks.
(1) you get 250 your logs *and* mail not coming in, try stopping any other antispam solution and/or running “iisreset” again. two servers, found the following the case.
This last step suggests that something awry with either Trend Micro’s antispam solution and/or this greylisting dll.
(2) 200 replies log might result blank entries the whitelist portion your Access DB. When you get 200 error, you still seem get mail, even spam. fix this, try deleting the blank entries and run “iisreset” from the command line. (200 reply “nonstandard success response”, see RFC 876).
(3) created file greylisting folder “fix” things when mail seemed mysteriously stop working. For whatever reason, that seemed work.
(4) Incoming mail from Gmail and Hotmail (dunno about Y!) might huge problems some environments. one server, new mail from Hotmail got bounced couple times, and new mail from Gmail would range from like minutes several hours (like 12). have suspicions why this the case (different IPs, different policies different servers, etc), but the bottom line that this could very frustrating for users and possibly lethal your job if, say, the CEO sent super important email from his gmail account someone the Exchange server. This criticism more directed greylisting whole, rather than GRYNX particular.
(5) have found this program 90% 100% effective. the server where was 100% effective, most the spam seemed dictionary attacks and/or email sent old domain that the Exchange server had its recipient policy (e.g. 12345@olddomain.com, etc). the server where was 90% effective, lot the crap spam seemed like stuff that users had subscribed (coupons, job board sites, etc). This type spam much harder deal with, seems adhere proper RFC standards.
(6) This solution good quick fix you’re pinch and need something fast and free. Ultimately, you’re running Exchange and need “free” greylisting spam solution that you’ll probably want put some sort box front your Exchange server. GRYNX very cool project, and while thoroughly appreciate the hard work that has gone into it, think that something like tumsgreyspf much better suited for mission critical environments (the admins use it, fact). GRYNX “works”, but only you’re willing fiddle and goof around bit before you roll out your production servers. you’ve got that much time burn, then perhaps consider implementing begin with.
the saying goes, fast, free, and good pick any two!
Thanks for your feedback this very valuable developing the new versions.
We’ve released new version under new name, JEP(S), and under another website, which addresses all the issues your feedback. sure check out!
(1) This longer issue JEP(S) handles all installation the sinks. Further there smart tool included (View sinks) which helps positioning JEP(S) correctly together with other mail add-on’s.
(2) Also longer issue, fixed JEP(S).
(3) longer issue, see
(4) longer issue. JEP(S) can use Realtime White Lists (RWL’s) which identifies especially large companies. When use then the SourceIP exluded the processing.
(5) also include tarpitting JEP(S) which protects against harvesting attacks.
(6) hope that you, after reviewing, see that JEP(S) lot more mature and that it’s more aimed the enterprise market without letting all installations smaller systems.

original text: with great sadness that announce the passing our beloved mother and grandmother Elly Sunday, January 20, 2008, the age years. She will welcomed with open arms her late son Pete, who passed away 1992, and her late husband Peter, who passed away 1998. She will fondly remembered and sadly missed her daughters Miriam and Jeanette, her sons-in-law Richard and Morad, and her grandchildren Mitchell, Lindsey, Erik, Troy, Cameron and Stevan. She will also missed her family British Columbia and the Netherlands. Wherever Elly lived, she became part her community, and always had many wonderful friends. Through her way caring for others, Elly touched the lives all who knew her. Her door was always open. Elly had full life, although wish could have had more years with her. The family would like thank Dr. Linda Fong, Sharon Paul, R.N., and the staff Rosedale Hospice for their dedication and support. Elly did not wish have formal service her memory. such, would like family and friends privately remember Elly, Sunday, January 27, 2008 4:00 P.M. with cup tea. Please take moment, sip your tea and quietly reflect who Elly was you. you wish your reflections will forwarded the family ccl@hffs.com, subject heading: Elisabeth Van Roosmalen.

original text:
Find group AAEM members your region who are dedicated the mission statement AAEM.
There must minimum 100 AAEM members the state region considered. Once this confirmed, you will need identify committed, enthusiastic EPs name for the board directors. Name president, and This will the Executive Committee. Then name board directors, anywhere from four six individuals. (Remember you want this even number that when the entire board meets for vote you have odd number when you add the Executive Committee, that you don't end with tie votes.) When the members the board are identified, you will asked submit form listing the board directors for the new chapter division. This will then approved the AAEM board directors.
This easy process you may use the bylaws template and make appropriate changes. The AAEM office will glad supply you with the electronic copy the template and assist you with this process. The bylaws must submitted the AAEM board for approval.
The chapter division board will sent operational documents for review and approval. Some require signatures from more than one executive committee member, please plan this process accordingly.
You will want buy copy Rules Order Dr. James Davis run the board meetings and conference calls. This edition was selected national AAEM due the ease use compared Robert's Rules. This book may found any local bookstore may ordered online.
have found that email very efficient way communicate. would encourage all officers and board members have email address. One two face-to-face meetings year are important, but having conference calls the interim helps the board take care business timely and less expensive manner.
You will want recognized your state medical society and get involved the legislative process. Your state medical society will able help you with this. suggest the president (at the very least) member the state medical society. They often have annual legislative leadership conferences, which you may attend find out what happening your state. This your opportunity meet and discuss issues local concern with state politicians.
what are you waiting for? Come on, throw your hat into the ring, and have great time doing realizing you will part history.
Contact info@aaem.org for more information get started.
